name: High-Protein Sugar Cookies
description: Low-flour protein sugar cookie tuned to kill the rubbery 'pancake' texture: one 33 g scoop of whey carries the structure with just 24 g AP flour, and SF instant pudding binds moisture for a dense bakery chew. Sweetener is granulated Splenda, trimmed to 0.5 cup (pure maltodextrin carbs, no protein — the cut is free calories); pudding is the Simply Delish SF vanilla; butter is regular unsalted, halved to 7 g. Leavening is baking powder only. On the house cookie protocol: 30-min fridge chill, press thick, bake at 330F and start checking ~8-10 min, pull pale and slightly underdone (Splenda won't brown), finish on the hot pan, then a fridge set. Optional pan-slap for crinkle (lower impact here than the butter cookies). Source: user. Computed batch macros ~428 kcal / 39P / 40C / 11F (107/cookie at 4; ratio 10.9).

steps.instructionText: Whisk the dry base — AP flour, whey protein, SF pudding mix, Splenda, baking powder, salt — until there are no protein clumps.
Add the melted butter, whole egg, Greek yogurt, and vanilla into the dry mix.

Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough (if still tacky, chill 5 more minutes). Then cover and chill the dough ~30 minutes in the fridge — same as the browned-butter cookies: a proper chill firms it for cleaner shaping and a thicker, chewier bake.

Preheat oven/air fryer to 330°F. Divide the chilled dough into 4 equal balls on parchment and press firmly with your palm or a glass to your target thickness — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread, so keep them on the thicker side for a chewy center rather than thin and dry.

Bake at 330°F. These are smaller and far leaner than the browned-butter cookies, so start checking at ~8–10 minutes to be safe — pull the moment the edges are set and the centers still look slightly underdone. They stay pale (Splenda won't brown), so never wait for golden tops: thicker-pressed cookies trend toward the 10-min end, thin ones can be done by 7–8. Optional, for crinkly tops and house-consistency with the other cookies: around the halfway mark rap the tray firmly on the counter once or twice (and again out of the oven) — note this does far less here than on the butter cookies, since there's only ~7 g butter and little risen mound to deflate, so it's truly optional.
Leave the cookies on the hot sheet 5-10 minutes; residual pan heat finishes the centers without making the protein rubbery.
Final step: rest in the fridge ~10 minutes to fully set, then eat.

notes.text: Whey dries out faster than AP flour, so this is a watch-it bake: start checking early (~8 min) and pull the moment the edges look dry and set — a soft, slightly-underdone center is correct and finishes on the hot pan. They stay pale because Splenda doesn't brown, so judging by color will overbake them dry.

The 5-minute rest after mixing is essential: the whey protein and SF pudding starches absorb liquid and the dough firms from tacky to shapeable without adding flour. Rushing past this step makes dough that spreads flat.
This dough does not spread on its own — press each ball firmly with a palm or glass-bottom to your target thickness before baking. What goes in is what comes out.
Leaving cookies on the hot sheet 5–10 minutes after pulling from the oven is the carryover finish — moving them to a cooling rack immediately stops this and can leave the centers underdone.
Pre-whisk the dry mix thoroughly to break up protein powder clumps before adding any wet ingredients — undissolved lumps don't hydrate evenly and leave gritty pockets in the finished cookie.

The 30-min fridge chill is the same firm-up the browned-butter cookies get: it hydrates the dough fully and lets you press a thicker cookie that bakes up chewy instead of thin and dry. Skip it and the warm, tacky dough presses thin and bakes through before the center can stay soft.
